title: Temperatures rise by up to 5°C in himachal ahead of light rain forecast from April 24 - The News Mill
full_text:
Temperatures in Himachal Pradesh increased by approximately 2 to 5 degrees Celsius in several districts over the past 24 hours, according to the Meteorological Department. Una recorded the highest temperature at around 38.4°C, while Shimla registered 25.6°C.
Districts including Shimla, Solan, and Kangra experienced a rise of about 3 to 5 degrees above normal daytime temperatures. Despite this increase, there have been no reports of heatwave conditions in the state.
The weather department forecasts clear conditions across Himachal Pradesh until April 23, with daytime temperatures expected to rise gradually by another 2 to 4 degrees Celsius in many areas.
A change in weather is predicted from April 24, with light rain likely in isolated areas, particularly in Chamba and adjacent higher regions. On April 25, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms is expected in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, Mandi, and Shimla districts.
From April 26, weather activity is expected to decrease, although some cloud cover and isolated rainfall may persist, especially in mid-hill districts such as Mandi.
The Meteorological Department noted that temperatures are expected to remain near normal over the next three days despite the recent rise. Officials added, “Current temperature levels in several areas, including Shimla, Solan, and Kangra, are already about 3 to 5 degrees above normal. For instance, Una recorded around 31.6°C, which is approximately 4 degrees above the seasonal average, while Shimla also recorded temperatures nearly 4 degrees above normal.” Overall, temperatures are anticipated to stabilise around average levels by April 24, even as intermittent weather activity begins.
Across India, contrasting weather conditions continue, with heavy rainfall, thunderstorms, hailstorms, and an intensifying heatwave affecting various regions simultaneously, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported.
The IMD stated that isolated pockets of Assam recorded very heavy rainfall (12-20 cm), and Nagaland reported heavy rainfall between 7-11 cm. Thunderstorms with squally winds of 50-80 kmph affected Marathwada, Odisha, Madhya Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, Arunachal Pradesh, and Assam & Meghalaya. Gusty winds of 30-50 kmph were also reported in several parts of Northeast, East, North, Central, West, and South India.
Marathwada experienced wind speeds as high as 83 kmph. Isolated hailstorms were reported in Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, and Telangana.
